# IMAGE-PROMPTS — Architecture Series

<!-- Maintained by docs/customer-documents authors. Pipeline:
     scripts/generate_images.py harvests [IMAGE-NN: ...] placeholders from
     each .qmd, refreshes this sidecar, and emits PNGs into ./images/. -->

## aodim-architecture — IMAGE-01 / 02 / 03

<!-- Originally generated by 05-seed-image-prompts.ps1 -->

## IMAGE-01

Technical architecture diagram showing the AODIM attribute flow: HR System arrow to Identity Attributes arrow to Dynamic Groups arrow to Access Policies arrow to Resource Authorization. Each stage labeled with governance checkpoints. Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) color scheme on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.

## IMAGE-02

Side-by-side comparison diagram: Left panel labeled 'Legacy OU-Based Delegation' showing a rigid tree hierarchy with Organizational Units and static group nesting. Right panel labeled 'AODIM Attribute-Based Delegation' showing a flat, dynamic model with attribute tags flowing to policy engines. Arrows show the transformation path between them. Clean engineering diagram, dark navy and teal color scheme.

## IMAGE-03

Operational flow diagram showing the AODIM runtime cycle: Identity Event triggers Attribute Evaluation, which feeds Rule Engine, which produces Access Decision plus Drift Report plus Evidence Object. Feedback loop from Drift Report back to Identity Event for remediation. Clean engineering style, dark navy and teal on white, no photographs.

## six-plane-architecture — IMAGE-01

Six-plane reference architecture diagram. Six horizontal lanes stacked
vertically, top to bottom: Identity (root namespace icon), Network (overlay
routing icon), Addressing (deterministic IPAM icon), Telemetry & Location
(signal-flow icon), Security & Compliance (Zero Trust shield icon),
Management (governance/CMDB gear icon). One-directional arrows on the right
side flow downward through all six lanes indicating canonical input flow. A
small recurrent feedback arrow on the left shows runtime drift findings
re-entering the Identity lane. Title "UIAO Six-Plane Reference Model".
Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C)
color scheme on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.

## six-plane-architecture — IMAGE-02

ADR-030 tier-A versus tier-B framing diagram. Left panel labeled "Tier A —
Four-Layer Prose Model" showing four stacked rectangles: Authority Plane /
Control Plane / Overlay Fabric / Physical Underlay. Right panel labeled
"Tier B — Six-Plane Decomposition" showing the six planes from this
document fitted entirely inside the Control Plane region of the left panel.
Bidirectional connector arrow between the panels labeled "same substrate,
different granularity". Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy
(#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) color scheme on white background. No
photographs, purely diagrammatic.

## drift-engine — IMAGE-01

Drift taxonomy state diagram. Central horizontal bar labeled "Canonical
Truth" in dark navy. Five vertical channels rise above the bar, each one a
labeled drift class (DRIFT-SCHEMA, DRIFT-SEMANTIC, DRIFT-PROVENANCE,
DRIFT-AUTHZ, DRIFT-IDENTITY). Each channel terminates in a small severity
badge (P1 red, P2 amber, P3 yellow, P4 gray) showing typical severity for
that class. Below the canonical truth bar, two detection-surface lanes —
left lane "CI Substrate Walker" highlighting SCHEMA + PROVENANCE columns,
right lane "Runtime Conformance Runner" highlighting SEMANTIC + AUTHZ +
IDENTITY columns. Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E)
and teal (#1E8C8C) accents on white background, with red (#C74040) reserved
for P1 only. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.

## drift-engine — IMAGE-02

Remediation contract flow diagram. Left side shows a "Drift Detected" event
entering an evaluator that classifies it as one of P1–P4. Three branches
emerge: P1 → Halt + Alert (immediate, red), P2 → Auto-remediate if
deterministic (1 hour SLA, amber), P3/P4 → Queue for auto-remediation (24 /
72 hour SLA, teal). Each branch outputs a "Remediation Contract" record
with timestamp, action, evidence hash, and escalation path field. The
records flow into a single output bin labeled "Evidence Chain (canonical
claim)". Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal
(#1E8C8C) on white background, with red (#C74040) reserved for P1. No
photographs, purely diagrammatic.

## evidence-chain — IMAGE-01

Four-stage evidence pipeline diagram. Horizontal flow with four labeled
stages connected by arrows: (1) Telemetry Event Capture — adapter boundary
icon, (2) Canonical Claim Normalization — provenance envelope wrapper icon,
(3) Bundle Assembly + Seal — sealed envelope with hash badge, (4) OSCAL
Artifact Emission — three small document icons labeled SSP, POA&M, KSI,
Component Definition. Below the pipeline, a parallel canonical-source bar
labeled "src/uiao/canon/" with three reference points feeding into stages
2, 3, 4 with light teal arrows. Each stage shows its canonical anchor
underneath (UIAO_PP_001, ADR-016, ADR-006). Clean engineering blueprint
style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No
photographs, purely diagrammatic.

## evidence-chain — IMAGE-02

Tamper-evident hash tree diagram. Top of the tree shows a "Bundle Hash"
root node. Below it, three "Per-Claim Hash" nodes branch out. Each
per-claim node connects down to a small "Source Record" leaf labeled with
extraction timestamp, source classification (authoritative / derived /
synthesized), and lineage_hash. A side panel highlights one claim's
transformation_chain as an append-only sequence of three steps. A footer
note reads "Modification anywhere breaks the bundle hash." Clean
engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) on
white background, with subtle red (#C74040) on the broken-hash example
only. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.

## three-layer-rule-model — IMAGE-01

Three-layer rule model stack diagram. Three horizontal layers stacked top
to bottom: top layer "Runtime (Enforcement)" — adapter and enforcement
runtime icons, middle layer "Policy (Mapping)" — registry and KSI mapping
icons, bottom layer "Canon (Rule Definition)" — schema-locked SSOT icon.
Each layer has a small badge on the right showing its change cadence
(Runtime: PR + tests, Policy: PR + schema validation, Canon: ADR +
governance review). Vertical arrows on the left side of the stack show
"read-only consumption upward" flowing from canon up to runtime; arrows on
the right side show "evidence and drift findings flow downward" back into
the layers. Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and
teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.

## three-layer-rule-model — IMAGE-02

Drift-class to rule-layer alignment matrix. Three columns labeled Canon /
Policy / Runtime. Five rows for each drift class. Filled cells indicate
which layer that drift class maps to: SCHEMA in Canon column, SEMANTIC
spanning Canon-to-Runtime as a long teal bar, PROVENANCE in Policy column,
AUTHZ in Runtime column, IDENTITY in Runtime column. Cells contain short
remediation hints (e.g. "schema regen", "registry edit", "code change").
Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C)
on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.

## boundary-impact-model — IMAGE-01

Boundary impact four-dimension diagram. A vertical boundary line down the
center labeled "Authorization Boundary" splits the page into left
("Outside" — commercial / non-boundary side) and right ("Inside" —
GCC-Moderate side). Four horizontal lanes cross the boundary, each labeled
with one impact dimension: Telemetry Fidelity, Identity Authority,
Enforcement Responsibility, Provenance Classification. On each lane the
boundary line shows a transformation badge (e.g. "authoritative →
derived"). Below each lane, a small drift-class badge indicates the drift
class that fires on violation (DRIFT-SCHEMA, DRIFT-IDENTITY, DRIFT-AUTHZ,
DRIFT-SEMANTIC). Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and
teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.

## boundary-impact-model — IMAGE-02

GCC-Moderate invisible-boundary visualization. Top half shows the M365
GCC-Moderate admin portal with five tiles labeled Endpoint Analytics /
Device Health Attestation / Locations & Network Fence / ARC Server
Monitoring / Expedited Updates — all displayed as available (green check).
Bottom half shows what actually functions inside the boundary — the same
five tiles redrawn empty or grayed out (red X), with explanatory captions
like "telemetry blocked", "planning phase", "explicitly unavailable".
Between the two halves, a horizontal "Invisible Boundary" line indicates
where appearance and reality diverge. Right margin shows four UIAO
compensating mechanisms (Boundary Probe, In-Boundary Telemetry,
Compensating Enforcement, ATO Documentation Surface) as small numbered
boxes. Clean engineering diagram, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C)
primary, with red (#C74040) reserved for the unavailable-state tiles. No
photographs, purely diagrammatic.
